title: Buyer Memory Ledger
problem: AI-native SME M&A depends on remembering buyer preferences, deal structures, and owner constraints without creating a black-box matching process.
direction: A buyer-preference memory and explainability ledger that replays synthetic sell-side mandates against acquirer criteria, explains why buyers were ranked, and detects stale or contradictory buyer memory.
why: DealFlowAgent publicly emphasizes conversational AI matchmaking and memory of buyer preferences. The project turns that claim into an auditable matching artifact Joe can inspect deal by deal.

archetypes.name: ai-native evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: ai-native signal changes while depends context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: depends boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: depends handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: remembering regression harness
archetypes.trigger: remembering behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: buyer operator packet
archetypes.trigger: buyer output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
